title: Unity of Reality: From Coordination Order (β=2/3) to Feigenbaum Chaos (δ=4.6692)
description: We present a rigorous algebraic derivation that unifies the universal constants of the Yakushev Unified Coordination Theory (YUCT) – the coordination exponent β = 2/3 and the algebraic invariants S_odd = 1.2, S_even = 0.8 – with the universal constants of chaos theory: the Feigenbaum constants δ ≈ 4.6692 and α ≈ 2.5029. Using the exact analytic relation 2α² = πδ and the YUCT‑derived approximation π ≈ S_odd·φ² (where φ is the golden ratio), we establish the fundamental equation 2α² ≈ (S_odd·φ²)·δ. This result demonstrates that the constants governing the emergence of complex order and those governing the universal route to chaos are not independent but are algebraically linked through the geometry of the circle and the golden ratio. We further strengthen the unification by analysing Wolfram's Rule 30 cellular automaton within the YUCT framework, deriving an exact expression for the growth of coordination entropy and analytically determining the critical depth t_crit ≈ 157.7 at which the central column becomes perfectly mixing. The small deviation Δπ = |π – S_odd·φ²| ≈ 4.8×10⁻⁵ is interpreted as a falsifiable prediction of YUCT, pointing to a measurable granularity of physical geometry. Experimental tests using gravitational wave interferometry (LISA), cosmological surveys (Euclid), and quantum interferometry are proposed. This record contains the complete multilingual version of the article (English, Russian, German, Chinese, Arabic, Japanese, Korean).

custom.notes: This is the complete multilingual documentation of the paper 'Unity of Reality: From Coordination Order (β=2/3) to Feigenbaum Chaos (δ=4.6692)', version 2.5. Each language file (EN, RU, DE, ZH, AR, JA, KO) is a full translation of the entire text, including mathematical derivations, tables, Python code, and references. The Zenodo community for YUCT is 'yuct'. The universal exponent β = 2/3 (0.6667) is explicitly included in the keywords for discoverability.
